ELPAC Level 3
Lexile ≥1400L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Stitched to Scale

STS-MS8-L3-1400P-MSMATH8-PZQ-IMR-02 • MS Math Grade 8 • Congruence and Similarity
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
Every family's journey on this quilt becomes a trail of shapes, and you unlock the next stretch of road by matching the right patch. One patch is an exact copy of another, with the same size and the same shape. A second patch is the same shape but stitched twice as big to fit the wide part of the map. Amara, Yusuf, and Mai are working the route from START to HOME, one matching shape at a time.
One pair of patches has the same size and the same shape. Another pair has the same shape but one patch is bigger. Which pair is congruent, and which pair is only similar?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Amara doing?
Look at the picture. Explain Amara's action and its purpose using precise language.
2
What is Yusuf holding?
Look at the picture. Explain what Yusuf holds and why the tool suits the task, using precise language.
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Explain what the teacher is holding and what it demonstrates about the shapes, using precise language.
4
What room is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Explain what room this is and justify it from the evidence, using precise language.
5
Tell me about the journey quilt.
Look at the picture. Explain what the journey quilt shows and its purpose, using precise language.
6
What is Mai wearing?
Look at the picture. Explain what Mai is wearing and her role in the scene, using precise language.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: CCSS 8.G.A.2 & 8.G.A.4 — Congruence and Similarity through transformations. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
